M/V ELEOVRYTISSA (ex-name: PARITY)

ICE CLASS 1C IS

LAST-NEXT SS: 3/22 - 3/27

P+I: THE U K

GRT / NRT: 24,168 / 12,156 MT
LOA / BEAM: 189.99 M / 28.50 M

AHL FITTED: YES

HATCH NO. 1 = 16.00M x 16.00M
HATCH NO. 2 = 22.40M x 18.00M
HATCH NO. 3 = 22.40M x 18.00M
HATCH NO. 4 = 22.40M x 18.00M
HATCH NO. 5 = 21.60M x 18.00M
 
TT STRENGTH: NO 1/3/5: 25 MT/SQM, NO 2/4: 22 MT/SQM
DECK STRENGTH: 2.5 MT/SQM
HATCVRS STRENGTH NO 1/2/3/4/5: 2.5 MT/SQM
STEEL COIL LOADING: 25T/ 2 TIER
 
SERVICE SPEED AND CONSUMPTION:
BALLAST: ABOUT 12.0 KNOTS ON ABOUT 17.0 MT VLSFO 380 CST PLUS ABOUT 0.1 MT LSMGO
LADEN: ABOUT   12.0 KNOTS ON ABOUT 18.5 MT VLSFO 380 CST PLUS ABOUT 0.1 MT LSMGO
 
IN PORT CONSUMPTION:
IDLE: ABOUT 3.0 MT VLSFO 380 CST PLUS ABOUT 0.1 MT LSGO
WORKING: ABOUT 4.5 MT VLSFO 380 CST PLUS ABOUT 0.1 MT LSGO
 
ABOUT TO MEAN MINUS 0.5 KNOTS ON THE SPEED AND PLUS 5% ON THE CONSUMPTION FIGURES BOTH IN THE OWNER’S FAVOR.
 
SPEED AND CONSUMPTIONS BASIS GOOD WEATHER DAYS DEFINED AS BELOW: MAX WIND FORCE BEAUFORT SCALE 4 (11-16 KNOTS) AND MAX DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 (TOTAL COMBINED (SEA AND SWELL) CORRESPONDING TO 1.25 METERS SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT) WITH NO ADVERSE CURRENTS AND WITH WATER DEPTH EXCEEDING 8 TIMES THE VESSEL’S DRAFT. FOR A STEAMING PERIOD TO BE CONSIDERED A GOOD WEATHER ONE, ALL TIME FROM NOON-TO-NOON HAS TO BE IN GOOD WEATHER AS DEFINED ABOVE.
 
ONLY VOYAGES EXCEEDING 48 HOURS TRANSIT TIME FROM SEA PILOT TO SEA PILOT - EXCLUDING A PERIOD OF 12 HOURS AFTER COSP/PRIOR EOSP - ARE TO BE TAKEN INTO ACCOUNT WHEN ASSESSING THE VESSEL’S PERFORMANCE. PASSAGES WITH RESTRICTED VISIBILITY AND/OR WHEN NAVIGATING IN CONGESTED WATERS, STRAITS, NARROW WATERS AND/OR WHILE FOLLOWING SLOW STEAM INSTRUCTIONS, AND/OR MANOEUVRING, ENTERING, OR LEAVING PORTS ARE TO BE EXCLUDED. FAVOURABLE CURRENTS ARE NOT TO BE FACTORED IN WHEN ASSESSING THE VESSEL’S PERFORMANCE. EXTRAPOLATION OF “GOOD WEATHER” PERFORMANCE FOR “BAD WEATHER” PERIODS IS NOT ALLOWED. VESSEL HAS THE LIBERTY TO USE ANY OVER PERFORMANCE TO OFF-SET ANY STOPPAGES EN ROUTE AND/OR ANY UNDERPERFORMANCE DURING ANY LEG OF THE CHARTER.
 
THE VESSEL SHALL HAVE THE LIBERTY TO USE MDO/MGO FOR MANOEUVRING IN/OUT OF PORTS, STARTING OF A/E, NAVIGATING IN SHALLOW/RESTRICTED/BUSY WATERS, CANAL, AND RIVERS. GENERATOR ENGINES CONSUME DIESEL OIL IN CASE OF PART LOAD. WHEN THE VESSEL IS PERFORMING BALLAST WATER TREATMENT, MANOEUVRING ON DEPARTURE/ARRIVAL, BALLASTING/DE-BALLASTING EXCHANGE AND HOLDS CLEANING WHILE AT SEA OR PORT THE TOTAL CONSUMPTION OF THE VESSEL WILL BE INCREASED BY ABOUT 4 MT. IF NECESSARY DUE TO COLD/HOT WEATHER PLUS ABOUT 0.8 MT VLSFO 380 CST FOR AUXILIARY BOILER CONSUMPTION.
 
IFO RMG 380 CST (VLSFO) STABLE AND HOMOGENEOUS NATURE SUITABLE FOR BURNING IN THE VESSEL'S ENGINES OR AUXILIARIES, NDAS TO COMPLY WITH STANDARD AS PER ISO 8217:2024 LSMGO TO COMPLY WITH DMA STANDARD AS PER ISO 8217:2024.
 
FOR THE PROPER BALLAST WATER TREATMENT, IN FRESH WATER PORTS, VESSEL SHALL BE ALLOWED TO CARRY ABOUT 250 MT OF SSW.
 
BIMCO ETS - EMISSION TRADING SCHEME ALLOWANCES CLAUSE FOR T/C PARTIES 2022 TO APPLY.
BIMCO FUELEU - MARITIME CLAUSE FOR TIME CHARTER PARTIES 2024 TO APPLY.
USTR FEES/ENVIRONMENTAL/GREEN/EMISSIONS TAXES FOR CHARTERERS A/C.

3) Steel Products
4) Cooper Concentrate
5) SBM & Maize IN BULK

9) Bayuquan, China (L)
10) Onsan, S. Korea (D)
